title: Intermediate: Model Building and Tuning
description: Move from concepts to practical model design, validation, and iterative improvement.

sections.title: Feature Engineering
sections.intro: Feature engineering transforms raw data into useful signals that improve model learning.
sections.example: For ride pricing, engineered features could include rush-hour flag, trip distance bucket, and weather category.
sections.exercise: Pick one dataset idea and propose two engineered features that could improve performance.

sections.title: Train / Validation / Test Split
sections.intro: A validation set helps tune model choices while the test set remains untouched for final evaluation.
sections.example: You compare logistic regression and random forest using validation scores, then report test score once.
sections.exercise: Explain why tuning directly on test data can produce misleading performance.

sections.title: Bias-Variance Tradeoff
sections.intro: Underfitting has high bias, overfitting has high variance. Good models balance both.
sections.example: A very deep tree can memorize training data, scoring high in training but poor in unseen examples.
sections.exercise: Give one strategy to reduce overfitting without collecting more data.

references: Adapted from public educational resources on model validation and overfitting/underfitting.
Adapted from openly available tutorial content on feature engineering basics.
